When the absolute difference of the two numbers is subtracted from the four-digit number so formed, the number obtained is 5481. What is the sum of the two two-digit numbers ?
explanation: As the larger number is written on the left, the larger number is either 54 or 55. Let the smaller number be x. Case I: The larger number is 54. 5400 + x = 5481 + 54 � x 2x = 5535 � 5400 = 135 (In this case x will not be a natural number) Case II: The larger number is 55. 5500 + x = 5481 + 55 � x 2x = 5536 � 5500 = 36 x = 18 hence, the required sum = 73.

So, there are 3 possible numbers.

S2 : If both the taps and the drain pipe are opened simultaneously, then the tank is filled in 5 hours 45 minutes. S3 : Pipe R drains out (fully) the filled tank in 15 hours 20 minutes. To know what is the capacity of the tank, which one of the following is correct ?

explanation: From S1 and S2 Volume of water filled in 1 minute by all three pipes together = 12 + 10% 6 = 16 ltrs Total time = 5 hrs 45 minutes = 5 x 60 + 45 = 345 minutes Capacity of tank = 345 x 16 = 5520 ltrs Form S1 and S3 Water drained by pipe in 1 minute = 6 ltrs Total time = 15 hrs 20 minutes = 15 x 60 + 20 = 920 minutes Capacity of tank = 920 x 6 = 5520 ltrs From S2 and S3 Time required for all three pipes = 5 hrs 45 minutes = 345 minutes Time required for drain pipe = 15 hrs 20 minutes = 920 minutes Capacity of tank = 920 x 6 = 5520 ltrs From S2 and S3 Time required for all three pipes = 5 hrs 45 minutes = 345 min. Area = y2, Perimeter = 4y Let x > y so that 4x > 4y ...(1) Given, x2 + y2 = 468 And 4x � 4y = 24 x � y = 6 y = x - 6 ....(2) Using (2) in (1), we get x2 + (x � 6)2 = 468 x2 + x2 � 12x + 36 = 468 2x2 � 12x � 432 = 0 But x being length cannot be negative x = 18 Put x = 18 in (2), we get y = x � 6 = 18 � 6 = 12 Sides of the two squares = x, y = 18m, 12m OR: Verify from options.

options: Statement (I) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (II) alone is not sufficient
Statement (II) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (I) alone is not sufficient
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but NEITHER statement alone is sufficient
explanation: Let the number be 23x and 23y Product of two numbers = Product of their HCF & LCM (I) & (II) imply the same thing i.e. xy = 12 Now possible value of x & y for which xy is 12 are (1, 12), (2, 6) & (3, 4). Hence we can find the smaller of two numbers by using either statement alone.

question: What is the value of a two-digit number ? (1) The sum of the two digits is 4. (2) The difference between the two digits is 2.

Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (I) alone is not sufficient
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but NEITHER statement alone is sufficient
Statements (1) and (2) TOGETHER are NOT sufficient
explanation: (1) x + y = 4 (2) x � y = 2 Solving above equations, we get x and y. But the numbers may be either 10 x + y or 10 y + x. Hence, we can't get the solution. Both statements together are not sufficient to answer the question.

subject: general_awareness
question: What was the population of State `A' in 1999 ? (1) Population of the State increases every year by 20% and its population in 1997 was 1,20,000. (2) Population of State A in 1997 was twice that of State B in the same year.

Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (I) alone is not sufficient
BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but NEITHER statement alone is sufficient
Statements (1) and (2) TOGETHER are NOT sufficient
explanation: (1) Population in 1999 = 120% of population in 1998 & Population in 1998 = 120% of 1,20,000. (2) We have no data of population of state B Statement (2) is not sufficient. Hence, only statement (1) is sufficient to answer the question.

question: If both the conveyer belts A and B are used, then they can fill a hopper with iron ore in one hour. How long will it take for the conveyer belt A to fill the hopper without conveyer belt B ?
options: (1) Conveyer belt A moves twice as much iron ore as conveyer belt B.
(2) Conveyer belt B would take more than 3 hours to fill the hopper without
Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (2) alone is not sufficient
explanation: Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but statement (I) alone is not sufficient BOTH statements TOGETHER are sufficient, but NEITHER statement alone is sufficient Statements (1) and (2) TOGETHER are NOT sufficient Suppose volume of the hopper = 3v From (1) : Conveyer belt A fills 2v of the hopper in one hour. Conveyer Belt B fills v of the hopper in one hour. Conveyer Belt A alone will take �< hours to fill the hopper. Hence, statement (1) alone is sufficient to answer the question.

subject: general_awareness
question: A, B and C are positive integers. Is their product an even number ? (I) A is an even number. (II) The product of A and B is an even number and that of A and C is also an even number.
options: Statement (I) ALONE is sufficient , but statement(II) alone is not sufficient
Statement (II) ALONE is sufficient , but statement(I) alone is not sufficient
BOTH Statement TOGETHER are sufficient , but NEITHER statement alone is sufficient
explanation: Since the product of any numbers is an even number if any only if at least one of them is an even number. Now, (I) A is an even number Product of A, B, C is even. (II) A x B is an even number and A x C is also an even number. At least one of them (A, B, C) is even Their product is an even number. Each statement alone is sufficient to answer the question.

question: A, B, C and D made their project presentation, one on each day, on four consecutive days but not necessarily in that order. On which day did `C' make his presentation ? (1) The first presentation was made on 23rd, Tuesday and was followed by `D's presentation. (2) `A' did not make his presentation on 25th and one of them made his presentation, between A's and B's.
options: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient , but statement (2) alone is not sufficient
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient , but statement (1) alone is not sufficient
BOTH Statement TOGETHER are sufficient , but NEITHER statement
explanation: Statement (1) and (2) TOGETHER are NOT sufficient. From (1) : We get the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th presentation date. (1) and (2) `A' did not make his presentation on 26th also, because `D' made presentation on 24th. Hence `A' made presentation on 23rd, `B' on 25th and `C' on 26th.
